name: French Onion Soup- Country Living
recipeIngredient: 1 large (about 1/2 lb) onion, very thinly sliced
1/4 cup Coarsely grated reduced-fat cheddar cheese
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: For best results, use a sweet onion, such as Vidalia or Texas Sweet, in this classic soup recipe.
When microwaved, these high-sugar onions cook more quickly and are less bitter than more pungent varieties.
1. In a 2-quart microwave-safe bowl, microwave butter on high (100 percent) for 20 seconds or until melted. Stir in onion and brown sugar. Cover with waxed paper and microwave on medium (50 percent) for 5 minutes.
2. Stir in undiluted broth and water; cover with paper and microwave on medium for 10 minutes, stirring after 5 minutes. Stir in pepper. (Soup can be made ahead up to this point and kept refrigerated; reheat before Step 3.)
3. Place bread slices on a microwave-safe plate; divide cheese among slices. Microwave on high for 20 seconds or until cheese is just melted.
4. To serve, divide soup among 4 small soup bowls; place one cheese-topped bread slice in each bowl and top with parsley. Serve immediately.
